items.title: South Korea's SK Hynix eyes US listing as soon as August, sources say
items.summary: STORY: SK Hynix reportedly plans to list its shares in the U.S. as soon as August.That's according to two sources familiar with the matter...The South Korean memory chipmaker wants to capitalise on strong appetite for AI-linked stocks and broaden its investor base.One source added the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission is likely to approve SK Hynix's American listing application during the week of June 22nd.SK Hynix said in March it had confidentially filed for a U.S. listing.A source said at the time the offering could raise as much as $14 billion.The company is the world's second-largest memory chipmaker and a key supplier to Nvidia.It's scored big in the AI boom due to its dominant position in high-bandwidth memory chips used in AI servers.Its share price has jumped 240% this year and its market value has topped $1 trillion since May.That makes it only the third Asian company after Taiwan's TSMC and Samsung Electronics to hit the milestone.An August debut would also add another top name to what is shaping up to be a busy second half for U.S. equity markets.

items.title: Crypto Weekly: Saylor's bitcoin sale, Greece mulls crypto tax
items.summary: STORY: From Michael Saylor's bitcoin sale to Greece's potential new tax... This is Crypto Weekly.:: Crypto Weekly:: Bitcoin hovers around key $60,000 markBitcoin hovered around the key $60,000 mark.But that hasn't stopped Standard Chartered's Geoffrey Kendrick feeling bullish about it.He's the lender's global head of digital assets research.Kendrick stuck with his call for bitcoin to hit $100,000 by the end of the year as most of the selling may be over.But he did call it a 'painful' week as the crypto token fell more than 12%.It also saw one of the highest profile holders of the token made a sale.Michael Saylor's Strategy said it had shed some of its holdings for the first in four years.Strategy is the largest corporate holder of bitcoin.:: Reform UK gets big crypto fundingReform UK raised more money than any of its political rivals for the third quarter in a row.That's what data from Britain's Electoral Commission showed.Nigel Farage's party saw big donations from billionaire crypto investors, including the Thailand-based Christopher Harborne.Reform raised around $12.4 million in the first three months of the year, while the governing Labour party took in just over $5.3 million.:: Greece 'prepares' crypto capital gains taxGreece could be moving towards taxing gains on cryptocurrency.That's what two government officials told Reuters.They said lawmakers are preparing legislation to impose a 15% capital gains tax on crypto.A senior government official told Reuters the Finance Ministry is readying a law that's expected to be submitted to the country's parliament in the coming months.:: Japan banks plan to issue stablecoinsAnd Japan's three largest banks said they will jointly issue stablecoins during the current fiscal year ending in March 2027.It would mark a major move towards digital payments in a country where cash and credit cards are still popular.Mitsubishi Financial Group, Sumitomo Mitsui Financial Group and Mizuho Financial Group announced the update.They said they will set up a council to examine the operational frameworks and prepare for the issuance.
